title: Parianella carvalhoi
intro: Parianella carvalhoi, also known as Brazilian ginseng, is a perennial plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, making it useful in the management of various health conditions such as arthritis and stress-related disorders. The bioactive constituents of Parianella carvalhoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to promote overall well-being and vitality.
taxonomy: Parianella carvalhoi is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Brazil. The plant belongs to the genus Parianella, which is part of the tribe Mutisieae.
